key_insight: A label-agnostic self-supervised learning pipeline can discover clinically meaningful tibial plateau fracture phenotypes directly from radiographs, avoiding dependence on inconsistent human labels.
engineering_relevance: This provides a reproducible AI workflow for fracture morphology characterization that can complement conventional classification systems and support more robust downstream decision-making.
method: Fine-tuned a RadImageNet-pretrained ResNet-50 with SimCLR on 154 cleaned knee radiographs
Reduced embeddings with UMAP and clustered them with k-means to identify four imaging-derived phenotypes
Validated phenotype coherence through blinded review by two independent clinicians
evidence_signal: The four clusters showed moderate bootstrap stability (ARI = 0.319 ± 0.041), strong cohesion (silhouette = 0.511), low overlap with Schatzker labels (ARI = 0.013), and one phenotype was unanimously judged to show comminution.
watch_item: The dataset is small and single-domain, so cluster stability and clinical generalization should be confirmed on larger, multi-center cohorts before deployment.
paper_title: Phenotyping TPF via Self-Supervised Learning: A Label-Agnostic Framework with Expert Validation
